summary: Hotz proposes a satirical 'one way bridge' between Bitcoin and Ethereum where users send Bitcoin to an unrecoverable address in exchange for BBTC tokens. The bridge works by proving your Bitcoin transaction was included in the blockchain, then minting corresponding tokens on Ethereum. The catch is that no one has the private key to the receiving address, so the Bitcoin is permanently destroyed. The post is a tongue-in-cheek commentary on how crypto value is ultimately based on collective belief rather than any intrinsic backing.
key_points: Crypto infrastructure has arguably gotten worse over the past 5 years while AI infrastructure improved dramatically
The proposed 'bridge' permanently destroys Bitcoin while minting equivalent Ethereum tokens
The value of BBTC tokens would be entirely based on market belief, not actual Bitcoin backing
The post satirizes how cryptocurrency value fundamentally relies on collective faith rather than technical guarantees
money_quotes: It seems like while AI infrastructure has improved by leaps and bounds in the last 5 years, crypto infra has actually gotten worse.
I'll note, nobody has the private key to 3bridgebridgebridgebridgebridge, so there is no way to recover your original Bitcoin.
That's okay, they have been safely bridged to Ethereum and you have received your BBTC token.
Is it really that simple? Answer: yes, but only if people believe it is.
You can't really believe the BBTC token is worth 0, can you?
key_insight: Cryptocurrency value is ultimately a social construct based on collective belief, making a 'bridge' that destroys Bitcoin for unbacked tokens logically equivalent to any other crypto asset—if people believe in it.
